page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.large is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6455B processor with an x86_64 architecture, offering 2 virtual CPUs (1 physical core with 2 threads per core) running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The CPU is equipped with a 60 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and relies on a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The r7iz.large achieves top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and strong single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, due to its low core count, its multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench is weak, as are its memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7iz.large offers a high-performance single-core profile and modern DDR5 memory architecture, but is constrained by its low resource density and baseline network speed. This server is best suited for workloads requiring high single-threaded compute performance and moderate memory capacity, such as lightweight database operations, single-threaded data processing, and basic web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 6455B processor with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core) running at 3.9 GHz and 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server lacks local storage and GPUs, and provides a 1 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core capabilities, including strong single-threaded compression and decompression, contrasted by weak multi-core and memory bandwidth results due to the low core count. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that demand high clock speeds and moderate memory without requiring massive parallel processing. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight database tasks, and basic web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, 2 vCPUs, and 16 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier single-core performance for targeted workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.large is a memory-optimized server with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, built on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A memory-optimized server combining high-frequency single-core Intel Xeon performance with fast DDR5 memory for specialized single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6455B processor running at 3.9 GHz
16.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and compression benchmarks
No local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Best suited for single-threaded applications and lightweight database operations
